# Transcrição: 02-More Customers

- **Fonte:** 02-More Customers
- **Duração:** 04:52
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:04]

So how do we go about creating more customers or more demand for our product or service? Now, you can't yet clone humans. That's not a viable way of getting more customers, but you can reach more people in different ways. The first one, which isn't always applicable to every business, is to go global. So if you're not already advertising outside of just one country or a limited number of countries right now, then scaling internationally is a great way of attracting new customers into your funnel when you've got a steady and ready business. So you've gone through the BPM method, you've done your business setup, your products and services, your fulfillment, everything's working. The next step of growth has to be, if it's possible, international expansion. Now, the international community is constantly growing in terms of the expenditure on e-commerce, digital products, etc. We are one big world right now as far as commerce goes. So global expansion has to be part of your strategy if your products and service can support it. And taking that back to a Facebook ads world, there are still pockets of opportunity around the world where you can still tap into lower costs and higher conversion rates in untapped markets. To give an example, I've highlighted Netherlands and Italy here. I remember a few years ago, we were pushing an Econ product and we're testing across different international boundaries to see where we could get traction. And Netherlands and Italy just stood out as low-cost, high-performing markets and we were able to scale within those. And that was done through testing and learning to see which markets would respond. And that's something which, when you go into your graduation testing process, I would create a new country-based campaign for this testing. If at some point you then want to expand into those countries, you can consider an account level expansion. So for example, I would generally launch a new account for global expansion when there's a consideration around time zone. So let's say, for example, you're advertising in North America because the US, let's say US and Canada is your call market, and now you're seeing traction in Europe, I would start a European ad account because of the time zone difference and the way the Facebook algorithm works at the basic level. There are many other ways, reasons to expand out into different ad accounts, for example, billing and reporting and other things which may suit your business. But that's something to bear in mind when it comes to international expansion. The other side of it is, okay, if you can't go international, it's actually thinking of other traffic mediums as well. So Facebook is great because there are billions of people on Facebook that we can reach on just Facebook alone. But you should never just rely

## [03:04 - 04:52]

on that one traffic channel. And I'm sure you're aware of it because that media space is not yours. You're paying Facebook to access the users on their platform. But if your business is 100% reliant on Facebook and anything goes wrong, and sometimes they do, for example, you lose your business manager, your ad account or anything else like that, even your personal profile will stop you advertising on Facebook. And if 100% of your revenue relies on Facebook, that's a problem. So because of that, traffic expansion is such an important part of growing any business. Doesn't matter what services or products you're selling, you have to be considering traffic expansion. Now traffic expansion is outside of this particular training program, but includes things like other paid channels like affiliate marketing, Pinterest, YouTube ads, Google ads, etc. Includes earned media. So for example, we're talking about PR and, you know, other opportunities around placing your product or your news story out there, and also other owned opportunities. So we talk about paid and owned media. Owned media would be things like a blog. So traffic generation by sucking in traffic from places like Google and Pinterest and other places like that into your funnel by using blogs. So there's lots of different ways to generate traffic in. But, you know, as you start to get great performance from Facebook and you start to get profitability and scale, don't wait for things to go wrong to look at traffic expansion. But equally, if you see an opportunity to grow your business, traffic expansion is another opportunity to expand your reach and get more people into your funnel to grow your business as well.
